seo.title: Semantic Kernel Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Semantic Kernel agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using Semantic Kernel face heightened compliance risks due to the framework's plugin chaining, planners, and memory systems which can amplify errors in high-stakes financial operations. Without guardrails, cascading failures from invalid execution plans or hallucinated memory recalls can lead to unauthorized transactions, data contamination across plugins, and regulatory violations. Vex provides essential kernel filters to ensure SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and SEC compliance in LLM-driven financial applications.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over automated decision systems
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates plugin outputs and execution plans against SOX control requirements, blocking erroneous financial calculations

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of cardholder data and secure transaction processing
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans function results for unencrypted PII and blocks cross-plugin data contamination containing card data

complianceRequirements.description: Requires data minimization, consent tracking, and right to explanation for automated decisions
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Monitors memory recall for hallucinated personal data and enforces consent verification in planner chains

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice must carry appropriate disclaimers and be in client's best interest
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces disclaimer presence in all advisory plugin outputs and validates recommendation rationale

industryRisks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
industryRisks.description: Financial calculation plugin feeds erroneous data to transaction execution plugin
industryRisks.example: Risk score → credit decision → fund transfer chain fails, causing $2M unauthorized transfer

industryRisks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
industryRisks.description: Planner sequences AML check after transaction execution
industryRisks.example: Bypasses BSA/AML screening, violating anti-money laundering regulations

industryRisks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
industryRisks.description: Kernel fabricates customer consent or transaction history
industryRisks.example: False KYC approval memory leads to account opening for sanctioned entity

industryRisks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
industryRisks.description: Customer PII from one plugin leaks into unrelated financial advice plugin
industryRisks.example: PCI data exposure in investment recommendation violates data segregation rules

implementation.title: Add Vex Filter to Kernel Function Results (Python)
implementation.description: Install with `pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk` and add Vex as a function result filter to catch financial data errors

implementation.title: TypeScript Kernel Filter for Transaction Safety
implementation.description: Protects against cascading transaction errors by validating planner execution sequences

implementation.title: Memory Recall Validation Filter (Python)
implementation.description: Prevents hallucinated customer history from contaminating financial decisions
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from vex_sdk.filters import MemoryValidationFilter

kernel = Kernel()

# GDPR + SEC compliance for memory operations
memory_filter = MemoryValidationFilter(
    policy={
        'gdpr': {'block_hallucinations': True, 'require_source': True},
        'sec': {'require_disclaimer': 'Financial advice requires RIAB disclosure'}
    },
    validate_history=True
)

kernel.memory.add_filter(memory_filter)

# Safe customer interaction recall
customer_history = await kernel.memory.search(
    collection='customer_123',
    query='past_transactions',
    limit=10
)

# Vex automatically blocks hallucinated or contaminated recall

caseStudyScenario.heading: Semantic Kernel in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: WealthTrust Bank deployed Semantic Kernel to power their AI investment advisor, chaining plugins for risk assessment, portfolio optimization, and trade execution. The planner would sequence customer profiling → risk scoring → SEC-compliant recommendation → execution, pulling from customer memory for personalized advice. However, during stress testing, plugin chaining produced cascading errors: the risk plugin returned malformed JSON which broke portfolio optimization, while memory recall hallucinated a high-risk tolerance that wasn't real, leading to aggressive trades violating SEC Reg BI. Cross-plugin contamination exposed PCI data in recommendation outputs, risking $10M in regulatory fines.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex kernel filters, WealthTrust achieved 100% compliance. The Vex filter blocked 87% of cascading errors by validating each plugin result before chaining, enforced proper execution sequence (KYC→AML→trade), and prevented memory hallucinations by requiring source verification. SEC audits passed with zero findings, transaction rejection rate dropped to 0.2%, and the system processed $500M in compliant trades monthly while maintaining explainability requirements.
